results.rendered_html:
ava-√yā (perf. 3. pl. -yayuḥ; p. gen. pl. -yātām)
to go or come down,  RV. i, 94, 12  and 168, 4 ;
(Ved. Inf. ava-yai) to go away (opposed to upa-yai, ‘to come up’),  RV. viii, 47, 12 ;
(aor. Subj. -yāsat; Prec. 2. sg. -yāsisīṣṭhāḥ [cf.  Pāṇ. iii, 1, 34  Comm.]; aor. 1. sg. -ayāsiṣam)
to avert, appease,  RV. iv, 1, 4 ; vi, 66, 5 ;  VS. iii, 48.

results.rendered_html:
avayā 2 U.
1 To go down, to give way.
2 To desist from, turn off or away.
3 To know, understand; athavā na dharmamasubodhasamayamavayāta bāliśāḥ  Śi. 15. 19. ; na jano'yamityavayaye sa tāpasaiḥ  Ki. 12. 15. 
4 To avert, prevent, remove.
